title: Thai Green Curry Paste

overview.description: A fragrant homemade Thai green curry paste made with fresh herbs, chillies and aromatic spices. Freeze in portions for quick homemade curries.

ingredients: 10 fresh green bird's eye chillies (adjust to taste)
2 stalks lemongrass, tender inner part only, sliced
Zest of 1 makrut lime (or 1 ordinary lime if unavailable)
method: Toast the coriander seeds, cumin seeds and white peppercorns in a dry frying pan over a medium heat for 2–3 minutes until fragrant.
Grind the toasted spices to a fine powder.
Place all the ingredients into a food processor or mini chopper.
Blend until a smooth paste forms, scraping down the sides as required.
Add the water a tablespoon at a time only if needed to help the paste blend.
Use immediately or divide into portions and freeze.

learning.did-you-know: Thai curry pastes are traditionally pounded by hand using a granite mortar and pestle, producing an exceptionally smooth paste.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to build a balanced Thai curry paste using fresh aromatics, toasted spices and shrimp paste.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: Making curry paste in batches produces far fresher flavour than shop-bought versions while making weeknight Thai curries almost as quick.
chapman-tips: Freeze in approximately 2 tablespoon portions—one portion makes a curry for four people.
Coriander stems provide almost all the flavour of the roots and reduce waste.
Use gloves when handling large quantities of bird's eye chillies.
If using frozen galangal or lemongrass, thaw slightly before blending.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze for up to 6 months in airtight containers or silicone ice cube trays.
notes.leftovers: Use to flavour soups, stir-fries or marinades.
